industry: Digital Personal Finance / Lending & Financial Services
thesis: SoFi is a digital personal finance platform with three segments: lending (student, personal, home), financial services (SoFi Money, Invest, Credit Card), and the Galileo B2B technology platform. FY2025 marked its first year of GAAP profitability (~$481M net income on ~$3.58B revenue, +35.6% YoY) with ~9.3M members. The Galileo platform offers high-margin, non-cyclical B2B revenue and is the key strategic option. The SPEC. BUY rating reflects strong growth but uncertainty around profitability sustainability, credit cycle exposure, and regulatory overhang.

drivers: Galileo accelerates to 40%+ growth, winning major B2B embedded-finance clients
Fed rate cuts expand lending margins and reaccelerate student loan refinancing
AI integration (Composer acquisition) drives Invest adoption and products-per-member above 2.0
Regulatory clarity under fintech-friendly administration reduces compliance burden

base_case.assumptions: Steady member growth (~25% YoY), sustained GAAP breakeven or modest profitability, Galileo growing 25-30%, lending margins stable. Stock trades at 4-5x forward revenue.

risks: Consumer credit deterioration triggers higher loan-loss provisions and compresses earnings
Regulatory action (CFPB, OCC) restricts product expansion or imposes fines
Fed hikes or prolonged high-rate environment suppresses refinancing demand and NIM
Competitive pressure from neobanks and legacy banks erodes member growth

catalysts.event: Q2 2026 Earnings — member growth, Galileo revenue, loan origination trends
Fed rate decision (July/September) — impacts lending margin outlook
Q3 2026 Earnings — first full quarter post-Composer integration commentary
Regulatory developments (CFPB/OCC guidance on fintech-bank partnerships)
Potential student loan refinancing volume surge if federal forbearance ends

key_risks: Credit cycle exposure in personal and student loan portfolios
Regulatory overhang from OCC, CFPB, and FDIC oversight
Sustainability of GAAP profitability through economic downturns
Intense competition from legacy banks and neobanks
Interest rate risk and refinancing demand sensitivity
M&A execution risk (Composer integration and future deals)
